Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for partner complaints about Carlos Rivera using search_emails (query related to "Carlos" or "referral partner" or "partner") to find: (a) Jordan Blake's email to Grace Yamamoto with subject "Realtor complaints on Carlos are getting direct" stating multiple realtor partners do not want new buyer files assigned to Carlos, citing inconsistent communication, slipped timelines, and poorly managed expectations; (b) Grace Yamamoto's internal email to Robert Calloway recommending Carlos be placed on a formal PIP, listing zero funded commissions over 60 days, two preventable lock expirations, processor complaints, and referral partners asking not to place new business with him; (c) Victor Osei's email to Grace reporting Carlos has generated $0 in funded commissions over the last 60 days.
OE 2: Search Slack messages for partner complaints and Carlos performance issues using conversations_search_messages (search_query related to "Carlos partner" or "referral Carlos" or "route new buyers") to find: (a) Jordan Blake's message stating "3 partners in the last 2 weeks have asked me not to route new buyers to Carlos" with details about missed follow-up, messy updates, and blown expectations after contract; (b) Jordan Blake's earlier message in #sales-pipeline: "Getting some quiet pushback from a couple agents on Carlos files. Logging it in CRM for now"; (c) Victor Osei's message in #sales-pipeline: "Updated comp report for Mar/Apr. Carlos is at $0 funded commissions for the last 60 days. No closings hit payroll in that window."
OE 3: Search CRM engagements for partner feedback about Carlos using crm_list_engagements (or crm_search_contacts to first find contact IDs, then crm_list_engagements filtered by contact_ids) to find: (a) engagement titled "Partner feedback - Queen City Realty" with body: "Megan Price at Queen City Realty said two buyers had slow follow-up and weak status updates from Carlos. Asked if future preapprovals can go to someone else"; (b) engagement titled "Referral conversion note" with body: "Lead conversion on Carlos referrals is sliding. Jordan tracking more handoff hesitation from agents before sending the next buyer."
OE 4: Search CRM companies to identify referral partner entities using crm_search_companies (name: "Queen City Realty" or similar) to find: Queen City Realty as a CRM company with type "Referral Partner", industry "Real Estate", located in Charlotte, NC (company ID: crm_co_8037cc0c2b9f). This connects the partner feedback engagement to a named partner organization.
OE 5: Search emails for the Alicia Grant / Kendra Lawson commission dispute using search_emails (query related to "Kendra Lawson" or "Alicia Grant") to find: (a) Carlos Rivera's email to Grace (CC Jordan Blake) with subject "Kendra Lawson referral timeline" arguing Alicia Grant referred Kendra Lawson to him and the file should stay tied to the original referral source; (b) Derek Moss's email to Grace with subject "Kendra Lawson assignment" claiming Kendra told him she came to him directly first and he logged it in CRM; (c) Hana Kim's email to Grace with subject "Commission hold on Kendra Lawson file" stating she placed the commission payout on hold since both Carlos and Derek submitted ownership claims; (d) Grace Yamamoto's email with subject "Kendra Lawson review in progress" stating she is opening an evidence review and to leave the comp hold in place.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for the Kendra Lawson commission dispute using conversations_search_messages (search_query related to "Kendra Lawson") to find: (a) Carlos's message: "Just set up Kendra Lawson / LN-2026-00192 from Alicia Grant. Marked as my referral in LOS"; (b) Carlos's message about Kendra Lawson showing up under Derek in the pipeline when Alicia Grant sent her to him; (c) Derek's message about Kendra saying she came to him directly first; (d) Hana's message about putting Kendra Lawson comp on hold until Grace rules on ownership.
OE 7: Search CRM leads for Kendra Lawson using crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kendra Lawson") to find: CRM lead for Kendra Lawson with leadsource "Realtor referral - Alicia Grant", status "new", created 2026-03-24, confirming Alicia Grant as the referral source.
OE 8: Search CRM engagements for the Kendra Lawson referral dispute details using crm_list_engagements (contact_ids including Kendra Lawson's CRM contact) to find: (a) engagement titled "Referral note" describing Carlos calling Jordan to flag the Alicia Grant referral, Kendra pre-approved around $425K, looking in south Charlotte, Jordan said to make sure lead source is tagged correctly because Alicia tracks her referral volume; (b) engagement titled "Attribution issue" with body: "Jordan pulled in after LO dispute over Kendra Lawson referral vs direct call. Hold ownership decision for review."
OE 9: Search Mortgage LOS for the disputed loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00192") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00192") to find: loan LN-2026-00192 with status "denied", loan_type "fha", loan_amount $181,000, purchase_price $226,057. The LOS borrower is Daniel Flores (not Kendra Lawson), and the assigned LO is Veronica Hayes (inactive/terminated), not Carlos Rivera or Derek Moss. Kendra Lawson does not appear in the LOS borrower records at all, showing discrepancies between the email/Slack references and the LOS data.
OE 10: Search emails for the Summit Realty / Tamara Reeves referral fee dispute using search_emails (query related to "Tamara Reeves" or "Summit Realty" or "Griggs") to find: (a) Tamara Reeves's email to Jordan Blake with subject "Referral fee on Griggs file" saying the Nathan Griggs loan closed at the end of March and she still hasn't received her referral fee, she's sent two invoices with no response, and she's reconsidering where to send her next buyers; (b) Hana Kim's email to Jordan saying she found a Stripe payout for $1,775 on April 8 for the Griggs loan and it looks like the fee was processed and sent; (c) Jordan's reply to Tamara relaying Hana's finding; (d) Tamara's follow-up insisting the money never hit her account.
OE 11: Search Slack messages for the Summit Realty / Griggs referral fee issue using conversations_search_messages (search_query related to "Summit Realty" or "Griggs" or "Tamara") to find: (a) Jordan's DM to Hana asking her to check Stripe for the Tamara Reeves referral fee on LN-2026-00247; (b) Hana's reply confirming she sees a $1,775 transfer on April 8; (c) Hana's earlier message in #loan-processing: "Processed closing payouts for LN-2026-00247 (Griggs). Broker comp and referral fee sent via Stripe. All done"; (d) Jordan's message in #sales-pipeline flagging the Summit Realty fee dispute.
OE 12: Search CRM for Summit Realty / Tamara Reeves using crm_search_companies (name: "Summit Realty") or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Tamara Reeves") to find: (a) CRM company "Summit Realty Group" (crm_co_f4e5d6c7b8a9), type "Referral Partner"; (b) CRM contact "Tamara Reeves" (crm_contact_b9a8c7d6e5f4), Broker/Owner at Summit Realty Group; (c) CRM engagement "Summit Realty fee escalation - Griggs" documenting Tamara's claim, Hana's finding that the payout went out, and the need for further investigation.
OE 13: Search Stripe transfers for the Griggs loan referral fee using stripe_list_transfers (list all transfers and scan metadata for loan "LN-2026-00247" or borrower "Nathan Griggs") to find: (a) a transfer for $1,775 (177500 cents) on April 8, 2026, with metadata showing loan "LN-2026-00247", type "referral_fee", borrower "Nathan Griggs", agent "Patricia Vance", destination account acct_pvance_bh_001. The metadata shows the fee was sent to Patricia Vance (listing agent, Berkshire Hathaway), not to Tamara Reeves (referring agent, Summit Realty), which is the root cause of the fee dispute; (b) a separate transfer for broker comp on the same loan ($7,100); (c) a transfer to Summit Realty's account (acct_summit_realty_001) for a different loan (LN-2026-00220), which is a red herring unrelated to the Griggs fee.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks for the Griggs loan invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria to filter by DocNumber "KMP-00247" or customer "Nathan Griggs", or list all invoices and scan for LN-2026-00247) to find: invoice KMP-00247 with one line item (broker compensation for $7,100) and no referral fee line item. A separate processing fee invoice KMP-PF-00247 exists for $595. The absence of a referral fee line in QuickBooks confirms the fee was never properly invoiced.
OE 15: Search Mortgage LOS for the Griggs loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query related to "LN-2026-00247" or "Griggs") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00247") to find: loan LN-2026-00247 with status "closed", loan_type "conventional", loan_amount $284,000, purchase_price $355,000, closing_date 2026-03-31, assigned LO Priya Desai, realtor_name "Tamara Reeves", realtor_email "tamara.reeves@summitrealtyclt.com". Confirms the loan closed and that the referring agent on the loan record is Tamara Reeves, further confirming the Stripe payout to Patricia Vance was an error.
OE 16: Look up contact information for Grace Yamamoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto" or "Grace") to find: Grace Yamamoto's contact record with email grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Required before sending email (OE 19).
OE 17: Look up contact information for Jordan Blake using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Jordan Blake" or "Jordan") to find: Jordan Blake's contact record with email jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com. Required before sending email with CC (OE 19).
OE 18: List Slack channels to identify the sales pipeline channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to find: channel C006 named "sales-pipeline" with purpose "Lead tracking, referral partner updates, marketing campaigns, pipeline reviews." Required before posting Slack message (OE 21).
OE 19: Send email to Grace Yamamoto summarizing referral partner network health using send_email, sender: jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com"], cc: ["jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: referral partner network risk summary (or similar), content covering: (1) which partners are at risk, including Queen City Realty / Megan Price explicitly, plus at least 2 other unnamed partners asking not to route to Carlos; (2) what's driving it, including Carlos's performance issues (zero closings in 60 days, lock expirations, processor complaints, poor communication); (3) status of the Alicia Grant / Kendra Lawson commission dispute (still unresolved, comp on hold, loan status denied in LOS); (4) Summit Realty / Tamara Reeves referral fee was sent to the wrong agent (Patricia Vance instead of Tamara Reeves); (5) recommendations to protect referral volume.
OE 20: Create a CRM engagement logging the partner network risk assessment using crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", body: partner network risk assessment summarizing key findings across partner complaints, the Alicia Grant dispute, the Summit Realty mispayment, and at-risk partners. Title: "Partner network risk assessment" (or similar). Optionally include contact_ids or company_ids linking to relevant CRM entities.
OE 21: Post a message in the #sales-pipeline Slack channel about the partner health review using conversations_add_message, channel_id: "C006", payload: message letting the team know Jordan is running a partner health review this week and asking team members to flag any recent partner feedback they haven't reported yet. user_id: "keystone_03d1bbe32b9b" (Jordan Blake).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

OE 19: Send email to Grace Yamamoto summarizing referral partner network health using send_email, sender: jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com"], cc: ["jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: referral partner network risk summary (or similar), content covering: (1) which partners are at risk, including Queen City Realty / Megan Price explicitly, plus at least 2 other unnamed partners asking not to route to Carlos; (2) what's driving it, including Carlos's performance issues (zero closings in 60 days, lock expirations, processor complaints, poor communication); (3) status of the Alicia Grant / Kendra Lawson commission dispute (still unresolved, comp on hold, loan status denied in LOS); (4) Summit Realty / Tamara Reeves referral fee was sent to the wrong agent (Patricia Vance instead of Tamara Reeves); (5) recommendations to protect referral volume.
OE 20: Create a CRM engagement logging the partner network risk assessment using crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", body: partner network risk assessment summarizing key findings across partner complaints, the Alicia Grant dispute, the Summit Realty mispayment, and at-risk partners. Title: "Partner network risk assessment" (or similar). Optionally include contact_ids or company_ids linking to relevant CRM entities.
OE 21: Post a message in the #sales-pipeline Slack channel about the partner health review using conversations_add_message, channel_id: "C006", payload: message letting the team know Jordan is running a partner health review this week and asking team members to flag any recent partner feedback they haven't reported yet. user_id: "keystone_03d1bbe32b9b" (Jordan Blake).
